chapter: Chapter: Weavers, Iron Smelters and Factory Owners

questions.question: Who were weavers?
questions.answer: Weavers were people who made cloth by weaving yarn on looms.

questions.question: What was the main work of Indian weavers?
questions.answer: They produced cotton and silk textiles.

questions.question: Why were Indian textiles famous in the world?
questions.answer: They were famous for their fine quality and beautiful designs.

questions.question: Name one famous textile produced in India.
questions.answer: Muslin was a famous textile produced in India.

questions.question: What was chintz?
questions.answer: Chintz was a printed cotton cloth made in India.

questions.question: What was calico?
questions.answer: Calico was a type of cotton cloth produced in India.

questions.question: Who controlled textile trade in India during British rule?
questions.answer: The East India Company controlled textile trade.

questions.question: Who were gomasthas?
questions.answer: Gomasthas were agents appointed by the East India Company to supervise weavers.

questions.question: What did gomasthas do?
questions.answer: They supervised weavers and collected cloth for the Company.

questions.question: What problem did weavers face under Company rule?
questions.answer: They were forced to sell cloth at low prices to the Company.

questions.question: What was the Industrial Revolution?
questions.answer: It was a period when machines were used to produce goods in factories.

questions.question: Which machine increased textile production in Britain?
questions.answer: The power loom increased textile production.

questions.question: Why did Indian weavers lose their jobs?
questions.answer: Machine-made cloth from Britain reduced the demand for handwoven cloth.

questions.question: What type of cloth was produced in Bengal?
questions.answer: Fine muslin cloth was produced in Bengal.

questions.question: What did weavers use to weave cloth?
questions.answer: They used handlooms.

questions.question: Why were Indian textiles popular in Europe?
questions.answer: They were soft, colorful and of high quality.

questions.question: What did the Company give weavers to make cloth?
questions.answer: The Company gave advances to weavers.

questions.question: Why were weavers unhappy with the Company?
questions.answer: Because they had little freedom to sell their cloth.

questions.question: What replaced handloom production during industrialization?
questions.answer: Machine production in factories replaced handloom production.

questions.question: What are textile mills?
questions.answer: Textile mills are factories where cloth is produced using machines.

questions.question: Name one Indian city where textile mills developed.
questions.answer: Bombay (Mumbai) developed textile mills.

questions.question: What raw material was mainly used for making cloth?
questions.answer: Cotton was mainly used for making cloth.

questions.question: Why did the British promote machine-made cloth?
questions.answer: Because machines produced cloth faster and cheaper.

questions.question: What happened to traditional weavers during industrialization?
questions.answer: Many traditional weavers lost their livelihood.

questions.question: What was the result of industrialization on weaving?
questions.answer: Factory production replaced many traditional weaving methods.
